methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terra Drone is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having completed an IPO on the Tokyo exchange. The valuation universe consists of five identified private peers—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and ACSL—with no public comparables available in the same narrowly-defined segment.

Traditional peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only four same-sector peers globally, and none meet the filtering thresholds for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or similar valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of drone and autonomous vehicle business models, capital intensity, and technology maturity across the peer set. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be derived from peer multiples.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without additional data on Terra Drone's financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, growth rate) or comparable transaction multiples. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue scale relative to private peers, unit economics in its specific drone application segment, and the market's pricing of execution risk at IPO. A complete valuation would require either disclosed financial statements from Terra Drone's prospectus or quantitative fundamentals from the private peer set to enable revenue or EBITDA multiple-based approaches.
